## ScreenShotPP v0.2.0

A big update focused on Windows capture reliability, a smoother selection
gesture, faster captures, and automatic update notifications.

### Highlights

- **Windows capture rewritten on Windows Graphics Capture (WGC).** The previous
  backend returned a black image for GPU-composited content (for example during
  a Microsoft Teams screen-share) and was unreliable on secondary or mixed-DPI
  monitors. WGC fixes both.
- **Drag to select from the top of a window.** A single click still selects the
  whole window under the cursor, but pressing and dragging (4px or more) now
  starts a free region selection even when you begin on the window's top band.
- **Automatic update notification.** On launch the app checks for a newer
  version and shows an in-app banner with Install / Later / Skip this version.
  "Skip this version" stops the reminder for that version until a newer one
  ships. A Changelog link opens the GitHub releases page.
- **Faster capture and correct Retina scaling.** The frozen capture is now
  displayed via an uncompressed in-process image (near-instant on 5K instead of
  several seconds), and window auto-selection is converted to physical pixels so
  it lines up with the Retina image.

### macOS

Download the `.dmg`, drag ScreenShotPP into `Applications`, then grant Screen
Recording permission on first launch.

### Windows

Download and run the NSIS `.exe` installer. The Windows installer is not
code-signed yet, so Microsoft SmartScreen may display a warning. Verify that your
download comes from this GitHub repository, then choose **More info** → **Run
anyway**.
